user_query:
I’m standardizing how our team handles alignment across domains. Please read these files under input/:
- input/org_context.yaml (team context and document owners)
- input/style_requirements.txt (editorial constraints)
- input/alignment_focus.json (domains to cover and priorities)
- input/servers.csv (sample data)

Then produce all of the following under output/:
1) output/taxonomy.yaml — a minimal knowledge taxonomy that covers the five document types we use (how-to/runbook, reference, explanation, decision, troubleshooting) and includes our core domains from org_context.yaml. Keep it structured and machine-readable.
2) output/runbook_cli_columns.md — a step-by-step runbook that shows how to turn input/servers.csv into a fixed-width, pipe-delimited table for terminal display. Use our runbook structure with Owner, Last verified (YYYY-MM-DD), Difficulty, Prerequisites, Steps (numbered), Verification, and Troubleshooting (with a table). Include at least one warning callout.
3) output/reference_memory_alignment.md — a concise reference doc about memory alignment (types, natural alignment, struct padding, cache lines). Use our reference template with Owner, Last verified (YYYY-MM-DD), Overview, a Type/Alignment table, and a brief change log section.
4) output/article.md — an internal explainer article summarizing cross-domain alignment do’s and don’ts (cover at least CSS, memory alignment, and either sequence alignment or typographic alignment, plus CLI/columns where relevant). At the very top, include SEO metadata lines: "SEO Title:", "Meta Description:", and "Keywords:". The SEO Title must be 50–60 characters and the Meta Description 150–160 characters. Use H2 subheadings, and include at least one quote with attribution (e.g., "...," said Name, Title). Follow the audience/tone guidance in input/style_requirements.txt.
5) output/automation_plays.jsonl — exactly three lines, each a JSON object representing an automation play. Fields per line: title (string), description (string), skills (array of strings), trigger (one of: cron|manual|reactive|event), effort (low|medium|high), value (low|medium|high), gotcha (string). Focus each play on alignment workflows relevant to our organization (e.g., aligning CLI outputs, CSS layout debugging, memory alignment checks).
6) output/servers_table.txt — a monospaced, pipe-separated fixed-width table built from input/servers.csv. Use a single header row and rows for each record; do not use box-drawing characters. All rows must align so that the '|' characters appear in the same column positions across all lines.
7) output/cli_alignment_cheatsheet.csv — a cheat sheet CSV with header: Context,Technique,Example,Notes. Include at least five rows spanning CSS, Memory, Sequence, CLI columns, and Typographic contexts, with concrete examples.

Make sure all files are self-contained and use US English. If you make assumptions, state them briefly in the documents where appropriate.
